summary: Rare Lovelight fragment with Neal Cassady rap
blurb: The only surviving audio from this July 23 1967 Straight Theater show is a short Lovelight excerpt, captured both as a soundboard snippet and a decent audience tape. Both recordings are incomplete but give a glimpse of the early jam and a spoken‑word rap by Neal Cassady.
review: July 23 1967 at San Francisco's Straight Theater remains one of the most elusive Grateful Dead performances, with no full setlist and only two fragmentary recordings to speak for it. The soundboard cut (gd1967-07-23.sbd.bershaw-wulf.5418.shnf) offers a clear but brief ~8‑minute window that features an early Lovelight jam overlaid by a rare spoken‑word rap from Neal Cassady – a historic oddity for any Deadhead archive. The audience tape (gd1967-07-23.aud.sorochty.125462.flac16) captures the same Lovelight passage with decent vintage fidelity, ending abruptly as the tape runs out. While neither source provides a full concert experience, together they preserve an otherwise lost moment of the band's formative improvisation and a unique cultural crossover with Cassady's narration.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described as having mixed sentiment – they note the rarity of the Lovelight excerpt but stop short of strong praise or criticism, resulting in a neutral adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source provides the clearest audio of the Lovelight fragment despite its incompleteness.

key_highlights: Neal Cassady spoken‑word rap over background music
Only two fragmentary recordings exist, both incomplete
